title: arctium lappaceum
intro: Arctium lappaceum, also known as the greater burdock or edible burdock, is a biennial plant in the daisy family native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. Arctium lappaceum is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, offering various health benefits such as improving skin and hair health, supporting digestive system function, and enhancing the immune system. Herbal preparations of Arctium lappaceum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made from its roots, leaves, and flowers, and are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
